summary: Tested new ZIP-driven places/search feature with curated fallback (GOOGLE_API_KEY unset) - both backend and frontend. 14/14 backend pytest cases passed; full frontend E2E flow (ZIP validation, cuisine filter, spin, reveal card, respin, reset) all passed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py logger is referenced inside google_places_search() before it's defined at module bottom - works only because logger is only used at call time; still it's a code-smell. Move logging.basicConfig/logger to top of file.

google_places_search returns raw Places API results without cuisine post-filter - if a real GOOGLE_API_KEY is added, request with cuisines=['Italian'] may return non-Italian primaryType results (query is broad text search). Consider filtering by prettify_type(primaryType) in cuisines when live path is used.
MAX_RADIUS_MILES=50 but locationBias radius passed to Google is 50000 meters (~31 miles), not 50 miles (~80km). Bias radius should be ~80467m to match the 50-mile promise.
seed_data_creation: None (Mongo seed auto-populated on backend startup with 12 curated restaurants)
context_for_next_testing_agent: GOOGLE_API_KEY intentionally unset -> /api/places/search returns source='curated'. Curated seed has 12 items across cuisines Italian/Japanese/Mexican/Indian/Chinese/French/Mediterranean/Steakhouse/Seafood/Burgers/Vegan/Cafe with prices $, $$, $$$. Zip is not used to compute distance in fallback (uses SEED-provided distance values). Frontend ZIP input strips non-digits and caps at 5 chars via onChange handler; cuisine chip testid is 'cuisine-filter-italian' etc.
